acceptance.assertion: The globe implements and tests planet, macroregion, region, local and focus states whose data forms become semantically more precise rather than merely larger.
Hue uses exactly the existing eleven-value Commons type vocabulary shared by filter, map, legend and text; no second active category system or blended type colour is introduced.
Coverage state remains a separate pattern and text channel; the current catalog is explicitly unassessed, and catalog absence or numeric zero is never presented as assessed Commons absence.

V1 uses deterministic derived spatial buckets from public geometry only. Any future source-backed aggregate-only public region context remains outside CommonProject until its provenance, privacy and zoom contract are separately proven.
Hidden locations never enter geographic geometry or buckets. Privacy-sensitive approximate aggregation obeys coarsening, minimum-uncertainty and k=5 release rules without exposing member identities.

The prepared catalog projection provides bounded planet and macroregion GeoJSON-equivalent aggregates, progressively replaced by existing truthful local points, approximate zones and public extents without per-frame reaggregation.

A compact touch- and keyboard-operable legend and a live textual geographic summary expose the same filtered identity set, type meanings, location precision and unassessed-coverage boundary without relying on colour alone.

Search and all intent filters update aggregate and local map layers plus text output from exactly the same visible CommonProject identity set, with deterministic caching and no anchor-based double counting.

Focused unit and contract tests cover geometry representatives, two-level buckets, identity deduplication, hidden exclusion, filter parity, coverage semantics, deterministic output, noninteractive aggregates, accessibility and performance guardrails.

Closeout binds full diff and SHA-256 to the exact PR head, records green required CI, identifies the exact merge commit, proves the matching GitHub Pages deployment and performs desktop and mobile/iPad browser readback of zoom transitions, colours, pattern, filters and text parity.

approval.note: User explicitly authorized registration, implementation, PR, merge, deployment and live readback for COMMONWORLD-GEOGRAPHIC-IMPRESSIONS-V1. Implement only a derived read-only geographic presentation. Do not add accounts, project administration, hidden-location geometry, inferred country membership or a second public write path.

goal: Make publicly evidenced geographic Commons visible from the complete globe through deterministic, privacy-preserving, filter-consistent semantic zoom, using the existing eleven Commons types for hue, explicit unassessed-coverage semantics and progressive dissolution into truthful public points, zones and extents.

does_not_establish: absence of Commons where the catalog has no record
a Commonworld administration or publication path
pairwise color discrimination without labels or patterns
metadata.observed_limit: The current public map exposes exact anchors, approximate zones and public extents only at regional/local zoom. It lacks a truthful planet-to-region distribution of the existing eleven Commons types and currently has no assessed geographic coverage basis or canonical country-region projection.
metadata.source: User-directed Commonworld geographic impressions and semantic color zoom implementation

title: Render truthful geographic Commons impressions across semantic zoom
